title: heliophila pusilla var. lanceolata
intro: Heliophila pusilla var. lanceolata, commonly known as Dwarf Nasturtium or Small-flowered Heliophila, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern region of Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Heliophila pusilla var. lanceolata typically involve making tea from the leaves or using the plant in a salve or tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
